description_long: This is a large, rounded ceramic vase with a narrow neck and two prominent looped handles on opposite sides, suggesting a classical or ancient design. The surface is marked by visible cracks and repairs, indicating that it has been reconstructed from multiple broken pieces. The coloration is a muted, earthy brown with lighter patches where restoration material fills the gaps. The handles are thick and robust, seamlessly attached to the neck and shoulder of the vessel. The body is bulbous, tapering gently to a small, slightly pointed base. The overall appearance is that of an aged, utilitarian vessel, possibly used for storage or decorative purposes in a historical or urban setting.
description: A large, bulbous brown ceramic vase with two looped handles and visible restoration cracks, giving it an ancient, reconstructed appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both looped handles and the full rounded body with prominent restoration lines.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a single handle and the extensive patchwork of repairs on the shoulder and body.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible, showing a more uniform coloration and fewer visible cracks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single handle and a concentration of restoration patches on the upper body.
